purpose: Monitor motor temperature to prevent overheating and enable thermal protection

outputs.name: motor_temperature
outputs.description: Measured motor temperature with degradation effects

states.name: sensor_accuracy_degradation
states.description: Temperature sensor accuracy degradation over time and environmental conditions

states.name: signal_quality_degradation
states.description: Sensor signal quality degradation from amplitude and phase deviations

states.name: actual_motor_temperature
states.description: True motor temperature being tracked by sensor

parameters.name: sensor_accuracy_timescale
parameters.description: Time constant for sensor accuracy degradation

parameters.name: signal_quality_timescale
parameters.description: Time constant for signal quality degradation

parameters.name: electrical_stress_factor
parameters.description: Electrical stress multiplier on signal quality

parameters.name: temperature_tracking_coefficient
parameters.description: First-order lag coefficient for temperature tracking

parameters.name: accuracy_error_scale
parameters.description: Maximum temperature error due to accuracy degradation

parameters.name: signal_noise_scale
parameters.description: Maximum temperature noise due to signal quality degradation

parameters.name: overheat_threshold
parameters.description: Motor temperature threshold for overheat alarm

parameters.name: sensor_disconnect_threshold
parameters.description: Signal quality threshold for sensor disconnect detection

state_transition_logic: sensor_accuracy_degradation decreases by (1/sensor_accuracy_timescale) * thermal_stress_factor * dt, clamped to [0.0, 1.0]
signal_quality_degradation decreases by (1/signal_quality_timescale) * electrical_stress_factor * dt, clamped to [0.0, 1.0]
actual_motor_temperature approaches baseline_motor_temperature using first-order lag with coefficient min(temperature_tracking_coefficient, 0.5), clamped to [-40.0, 200.0]
output_logic: motor_temperature = actual_motor_temperature + accuracy_error_scale * (1 - sensor_accuracy_degradation) + signal_noise_scale * (1 - signal_quality_degradation)
assumptions: Sensor accuracy degrades primarily due to thermal cycling and environmental exposure
Signal quality degradation affects measurement noise but not systematic bias
Motor temperature follows a baseline operating profile with thermal lag
Sensor disconnect is detectable through signal quality monitoring
Temperature measurement errors increase linearly with degradation states
